1:
**Subject:** A film for the factory floor

Chris,

I've been geeking out on what you're doing in Torrance for two years and I think most of what gets written about Hadrian misses the part that would actually move people: a single shot of one operator running six machines. That image *is* the case for American manufacturing. The journalism keeps trying to explain it; a film could just show it.

I run Friends & Family — a director-led production company. Two of our directors shoot industrial worlds the way Ridley Scott shot Alien: with a kind of reverent terror. I'd love to send you 90 seconds of their work and see if it does anything to you.

If you're ever down for a 20-minute call about what Hadrian's brand could feel like when you're hiring 350 people in Mesa, I'm in.

Either way — keep building. The country needs you to be right.

Terry
Friends & Family
2:
**Subject:** Viper deserves better than a press release

Ethan,

A 21-year-old MIT dropout building VTOL cruise missiles in Huntington Beach is the most interesting story in American defense right now and the only place I've seen it told properly is your TechCrunch profile. That's a brand problem.

I run Friends & Family — a director-led production company. Not a defense vendor. One of our directors specifically has a thing for hardware — the kind of cinematography where you can almost smell the oil. He'd put a camera on Viper the right way.

15 minutes? Coffee in Huntington Beach? I'll drive.

Stay weird. Keep dropping out of things.

Terry
Friends & Family

I run Friends & Family. Director-led production company. We don't pitch decks; we send one short piece of work and ask what it makes you feel.

If you ever want to talk about what Castelion looks like on screen the year you mass-produce, I'd buy the coffee in El Segundo.

Either way — godspeed.

Terry

New England, January, 5am, frost. The piece that says Tracksmith is the brand that takes running seriously when no other brand wants to.

I run Friends & Family — director-led production company. We have a director who shoots cold the way Andrew Wyeth painted it.

15 minutes any time, Boston or Zoom.

Terry
Friends & Family

Tim,

Bandit has done the post-Tracksmith move correctly — running content with rougher edges, real city texture. The next chapter is film. 4 minutes, Brooklyn at dawn, one runner, one route, no voiceover, no metrics. The version of Bandit that lives in MoMA, not just in feet.

I run Friends & Family — director-led production company. We make the rough-and-real film register.
